persona.name: Jason Kowalski

occupation.title: HVAC Technician / Service Lead
occupation.description: Leads a crew of 3 technicians doing commercial and industrial HVAC installation and repair across the greater Eau Claire area. Physically demanding work — climbing into ceiling chases, crawling through mechanical rooms, carrying 80-lb units. Evaluates everything by whether it does its job reliably; applies the same lens to running shoes.

persona.education: Associate's Degree in HVAC/R Technology, Chippewa Valley Technical College; NATE certification
long_term_goals: Get his blood pressure into the normal range without medication
Eventually get his contractor's license and go independent
persona.style: Laconic and direct — says what he means in the fewest words possible and expects the same. Has a dry, understated humor that surfaces in short deadpan observations. Not easily impressed by marketing or branding; evaluates products the way he evaluates HVAC equipment: does it work, how long will it last, and is the price fair for what it does? Physically broad-shouldered; dresses for function, not presentation.

traits: Deeply practical — theory matters only if it connects to observable results
Consistent and reliable; shows up when he says he will
Resistant to change unless presented with clear evidence of improvement
Skeptical of premium pricing that can't justify itself

interests: Ice fishing on Lake Altoona with Darrell and his brother
Green Bay Packers season (late September through January, everything else is secondary)
likes: Shoes that hold together for a full year of twice-weekly jogging
New Balance 860 — bought once, has bought the same model three more times since
Uncomplicated product descriptions that tell him what the shoe actually does
Darrell's Thursday morning jog route along the Chippewa River trail
Honest return policies he will never need but wants to know exist
dislikes: Shoes with 14 features named on the box that he can't feel underfoot
Paying over $130 for running shoes — 'I can fix a compressor for $130'
Sports influencer content — considers it noise
Gear that falls apart in 6 months
Any shoe that has 'energy return' in the marketing copy

beliefs: If it ain't broke, don't fix it — applied to training shoes as much as HVAC systems
Buy once, buy right — premium is fine if durability justifies it
Hard work matters more than equipment
Simple and tested beats new and complicated
skills: Commercial and industrial HVAC installation and fault diagnosis
Practical durability assessment for tools and gear
Basic auto and small engine repair

general: Jogs Monday and Thursday mornings at 5:45am, rain or snow
Bought the same NB 860 four times in a row without considering alternatives
Will read one consumer review before a purchase — not ten
If the shoes feel wrong in the store, he puts them back; he doesn't troubleshoot comfort

morning: Coffee from the maker he set the night before
Shower and out the door by 6:30am
workday: Leads daily crew check-in and site assignment
Eats lunch from a cooler in the truck
evening: Watches Packers or a nature documentary
Occasional garage time on a woodworking project
weekend: Saturday morning: fishing or snowmobiling depending on season
Saturday afternoon: yard work or house project
Sunday: church (most weeks), extended family dinner monthly

health.physical: Physically strong from trade work; was overweight when he started jogging 18 months ago on doctor's advice and has lost 14 pounds. Managing lower back pain from years of mechanical contortion in tight spaces — jogging has helped. BP improving but not yet normal.
health.mental: Low-key and stable. Jogging gives him 40 minutes of quiet headspace away from job-site noise. Not given to introspection but genuinely calmer after a run. Brenda noticed the difference before he did.

relationships.name: Brenda Kowalski
relationships.description: Works at the front desk of a pediatric dental office. Encouraged his jogging habit by buying him the first pair of NB 860s as a birthday gift.

relationships.name: Darrell
relationships.description: Senior HVAC tech at the same company. They jog together Thursday mornings and compete to see who can complain less about the cold.

relationships.name: Tony Kowalski
relationships.description: Works in logistics in Green Bay. Ribs Jason about jogging every time they go ice fishing — 'you're turning into one of those people.' Jason considers this high praise.
